food_subgroup: Processed chicken products — breaded and fried nuggets with cured pork and creamy dressing, served as a meal set

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002490 covers chicken nugget as a food product. The full meal set including bacon and ranch dressing is not individually coded; closest_parent applied.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers breaded fried chicken nuggets. The composite meal with bacon and ranch dressing is not individually represented; closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for this composite fast-food meal product.

serving.description: 1 meal set (approximately 350 g including nuggets, bacon, ranch dressing, and side)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard meal serving of approximately 350 g, comprising breaded chicken nuggets topped with bacon pieces and ranch dressing, typically served with a side item such as a drink or fries.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken nuggets, breaded, cooked — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 21140); supplemented with USDA Branded Food data for bacon and ranch dressing components
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA or HPB entry covers this exact composite meal. Values are estimated by weighted combination of: (1) USDA SR Legacy NDB 21140 — chicken nuggets, breaded, cooked (primary base, ~70% weight); (2) USDA SR Legacy NDB 10124 — bacon, cooked (~10% weight); (3) USDA SR Legacy NDB 04135 — ranch dressing (~15% weight); (4) residual side components (~5%). Per-100g values represent the blended composite. This is a medium-confidence estimate.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; analogous estimate based on breaded fried chicken products and refined-carbohydrate coating
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific branded meal. GI of 55 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for breaded fried chicken products and refined wheat-flour coatings, which fall in the low GI range (GI <= 55). Ranch dressing and bacon contribute negligible glycemic load. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 58.8) / 100 ≈ 32.3, where carbohydrate per 350 g serving = 16.8 × 3.5 = 58.8 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~520 mg per 100 g; ~1820 mg per meal serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this meal due to its very high sodium content, which substantially exceeds the recommended 600 mg per meal threshold for blood pressure management.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (GI 55) but high glycemic load per serving (~32.3) due to large carbohydrate content (~58.8 g), combined with high fat content that may impair postprandial insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should exercise caution with this meal as the high glycemic load (~32.3) and elevated saturated fat content can contribute to postprandial hyperglycaemia and reduced insulin sensitivity; portion control and pairing with low-GI foods is strongly advised, despite the low GI of the food itself.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (3.8 g/100 g), trans fat (0.2 g/100 g), high sodium, and cholesterol from bacon and fried coating
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This meal is high in saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium — all established cardiovascular risk factors — and should be consumed only occasionally and in reduced portions by individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Bacon Ranch Nuggets Meal is a Western-style fast-food meal set consisting of breaded and deep-fried chicken nuggets topped or served alongside crispy bacon pieces and creamy ranch dressing, typically accompanied by a side item such as a beverage or fries. The chicken nuggets are made from processed chicken breast meat coated in a seasoned breadcrumb or batter crust and deep-fried until golden and crispy. Bacon adds a smoky, salty, and savoury dimension, while ranch dressing — a buttermilk-based creamy condiment flavoured with herbs such as dill, chives, and garlic — provides a cool, tangy contrast. The overall flavour profile is rich, salty, and indulgent. In Singapore, this meal is available through quick-service restaurant chains such as Subway and similar Western fast-food outlets on delivery platforms including field survey. Nutritionally, the meal is energy-dense (~265 kcal per 100 g), high in fat (16.2 g/100 g), moderate in protein (14.5 g/100 g), and contains significant sodium (~520 mg/100 g), making it a high-concern food for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic load per serving is high (~32), driven by the refined carbohydrate coating on the nuggets. This meal is ultra-processed and not suitable for frequent consumption as part of a health-promoting diet.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry covers this exact composite branded meal. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted combination of USDA SR Legacy entries for chicken nuggets (NDB 21140), bacon (NDB 10124), and ranch dressing (NDB 04135). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for breaded fried chicken products. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are high-quality USDA SR Legacy entries and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates derived from USDA SR Legacy component entries (chicken nuggets NDB 21140, bacon NDB 10124, ranch dressing NDB 04135) weighted by approximate meal composition. Direct laboratory analysis of this specific branded product is not available in any approved primary source. 2. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entries used for this composite estimate. 3. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not consistently reported across the component USDA SR Legacy entries used. 4. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entries for ranch dressing and bacon used in this composite. 5. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for breaded fried chicken products; no direct GI measurement exists for this branded meal. GI 55 falls into the 'low' category. 6. Halal status is marked false as bacon (pork) is a primary ingredient; this meal is not suitable for halal dietary requirements. 7. The meal is served at Subway Funan and Funan Mix & Match outlets in Singapore as recorded in field survey data data. 8. Trans fat value of 0.2 g/100 g is estimated from USDA data for commercially fried breaded chicken products and may vary by preparation method and oil type.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected `gi_category` from 'medium' to 'low' to accurately reflect the `glycemic_index` of 55, which falls within the low GI range (<=55).

Updated the `key_factor` and `guidance` for `type2_diabetes` in `health_context` to align with the corrected 'low' GI category while maintaining the high concern level due to high glycemic load and fat content.
Updated `veritas_meta.iteration_count_used` to 2 and `veritas_meta.generated_at` to current timestamp.
